name: Tofu Provencal
recipeIngredient: 35 oz Canned plum tomatoes with juice (chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a bit of oil in a large skillet. Stir fry tofu until golden brown.
2. Remove the fried tofu from the pan and drain any excess oil.
3. In the same skillet, sauté garlic and onions for about 5 minutes.
4. Add tomatoes (with juice) and tomato paste to the pan. Stir until everything is well blended.
5. Return the fried tofu to the pan. Add the remaining ingredients and toss thoroughly.
6. Cover the pan and simmer on low heat for 15 minutes, or until the green beans are tender. If the sauce is too thin, reduce it uncovered by boiling off some of the liquid until desired consistency is reached.
We LOVE this quick simple dish; sometimes I will substitute zucchini, or squash, or mushrooms for all or part of the tofu. Enjoy.
A quick easy companion dish:
One box of NEAR EAST Bulghur Pilaf and some fresh mushrooms.
Slice the mushrooms and add them to a saucepan with water needed for the Pilaf. Bring to a boil, then add the mix and simmer for 15 minutes. It is GREAT! From Quick Vegetarian Pleasures by Jeanne Lemlin (an EXCELLENT everyday cookbook)
